column5: Description + Effect

Ballistic Helmet in use by the Russian Military. 
Has Helmet Mount for NVG Peripherals. [Armor Class 3]

Lightweight Helmet made in Russia. 
Inspired greatly by the Ops-Core FAST Helmet, but to a lesser, more cheaper degree. [Armor Class 3]

Russian Ballistic Helmet with Covering. Does not support headphones, and limits hearing. 
Its weight is alot lighter however compared to other helms. [Armor Class 3]

A similar model to the Kiver, featuring event stronger materials to ensure maximum protection. 
Heavier as a result. [Armor Class 3]

column1: MSA ACH TC-2002 MICH Series Helm
column5:
Modular ACH Helm, providing ample protection and comfort. 
Low Profile Design allows usage of NVGs without weighing the user down. [Armor Class 3]

column1: MSA Gallet TC 800 High Cut Helm
column5:
Ballistic Helmet in use by Police Forces and Special Forces alike. 
Highly Modular Design allows for additional armor plating to be applied. [Armor Class 3]

A simple, yet effective modular helmet akin to similar choices. 
Features less customizability, but is effective at protecting the user from a closer range. [Armor Class 3]

Extremely customizable ACH Platform favored by Special Forces around the world. 
With good ballistics all-around, it's a very safe bet. [Armor Class 4]

Unique Helmet Shape improves comfort for the user, all while upholding protection. 
With additional modifcations available, the Airframe can be tweaked to any need. [Armor Class 4]

Recent Tactical Helmet Design out of the recent few years. 
Similar to that of the Ops-Cor FAST Helm, this helmet builds further upon user protection. 
Highly Rated across the board. [Armor Class 4]

Lightweight Modular Helmet that prioitizes user weight and speed over customizability. 
Lighter than other helms, all without the loss of protection quality. [Armor Class 4]

Definitive Russian Heavy Ballistics Helmet, utilized the Spetznas Forces in 1980. 
Features a clear sightline visor and maximum protection. Heavy and bulky. [Armor Class 5]

Allows Communication between Members. 
Not much else aside from that.

An improvement on the Altyn Helmet utilizing different materials. 
Provides even better protection than the other and extends its durability even more. [Armor Class 5]

The ultimate in Head Protection, the Vulkan-5 is at the top of it's class in protection, bar none.
Featuring an exceedingly strong face-visor and good material ballistics, [Armor Class 5]
Description + Effect

Allows Communication between Members. 
Not much else aside from that.

Bare-minimum Ops-Core FAST Headset that allows for the amplifcation of sounds around the user. 
Acceptable for it's weight, but it's amplifcation leaves alot to be desired.

Common Use Amplification Headset used across the world. 
Tried and tested, this headset is a reliable piece of equipment, light, and allows communication through radio.

Russian-made Headset made to compete with the Ops Core FAST headset. 
Provides a more secure head strap, at the cost of comfort to an extent. Reliable nonetheless without comms.

New Design Specification to be Field Tested, the Arc III aims to provide clear communications between squad members
without sacrificing the utility of sound amplification.

A headset built upon the importance of sound over communication. 
Using experimental tech, the lack of a traditional amp cord lowers weight, but provides some amplifcation through design.

Tactical Headset specializing in ballistic support. 
Provides no such amplifcation of sound, but can be plugged into a radio unit to hear comms.

Amplification Headset in use by the US Military. 
Provides the best in surround amplifcation sound design, all while being comfortable to use. 
Can be mounted onto any Ops-Core FAST Helms.

Sound Amplification and Dampening Headset developed by Shironome R&D. Curated from multiple sources, Many currently
employed by the company have greatly praised it's comfort and usage on the field.

Large-headset specializing in ballistics protection. 
There is some sembelance of sound amplification, but it is rather poor compared to others.

Headphones used primarily to protect the user's hearing. 
Useful primarily in CQC Enviroments, where sounds are not too sensitive.

New Brand Digital Headset, specializing in communications and ballistics protection. 
Used in multiple SWAT teams in the Americas as of late.

Improving upon an already great design, the ComTac 4 is the be-all, end all headphone set, providing excellent sound amplification
and dampening, while operating on it's own battery pack, nullifying electronic pulse distruptions.
Description + Effect

An ordinary scarf. Adds resistance against gas. 
Can be worn half-faced, or full cover. 
Comes in a variety of colors.

Heavy-Duty Ballistic Facemask. 
For taking on challanges head on. 
Incompatible with Helmets, but strong enough to withstand some shots before breaking.

Neckwear primarily for user comfort and warmth. 
Calms the nerves, and keeps body tempertures around the neck regulated.

Negates effect of Hazardous Gas, but limits field of vision slightly. 
Applicable to Armor Class 3 and 4 Helmets.
The Visor Tint is Customizable.

Full on Facecover, with holes around the eyes and mouth. 
Keeps the face warm, and removes the uncomfortable feeling of breathing through a full balaclava.

Keeps the user's face warm with special technological materials. 
Slightly uncomfortable outside of extreme enviroments.

Ballistic Face-mask. 
Extremely limits vision of the user in turn for face protection. [Armor Class 4]
[Armor Class 3]

Ballistic Glass Visor. 
Extremely strong and used to negate most impacts towards the face. [Armor Class 5]

Face-Mask featuring a goggles like visor. 
Gives a good bit of perspective and is decent at face protection. [Armor Class 4]
Similar to the Altyn Face Shield, but heavier, providing better protection to the user. [Armor Class 5]
Side-panels for the AirFrame Helmet. Provides some protection to the face. [Armor Class 3]
Ear-panels for the Ops-Core FAST Helmet without sacrificing headset compatability. [Armor Class 4]

Ballistic Half-Mask for use in Ops-Core FAST Helmets. 
The design is sleek, and provides protection without compromising comfort. [Armor Class 4]
Description + Effect

Expertly crafted pointman glasses for the head of the squad. 
Comfortable and reliable. High Resistance to Flashbangs
Update to the Radio System. Supports on-screen map display..

Mass produced Military Specs with flash resistance in mind. 
Slightly uncomfortable to wear. Low Resistance to Flashbangs

Mass produced Military Specs with flash resistance in mind. 
Conforms to the face nicely. Low Resistance to Flashbangs
Oakley-made Glasses. The crossbow's less pricey option. Medium Resistance to Flashbangs.

Tactical Goggles made for the Russian Military. 
Provides a sembelance of ballistic protection, and protects the eyes from weather conditions.

Widely Used Military Goggles utilized by a number of military powers. 
Doesn't support ballistic protection, but provides semi protection from flashbangs and hazardous gas.
Run of the Mill Specs run by Special Forces around the world. Medium Resistance to Flashbangs.

A pair of Aviators. Though not designed with flashbang resistance in mind, it does the job all the same. 
These are famous. Medium Resistance to Flashbangs

A pair of Hipster Glasses, modified with special lenses. 
For those DIY Nerds. Medium Resistance to Flashbangs

Tinted Ballistic Goggles often utilized by American Military Servicemen. Doubling as a pair of sunglasses makes it rather popular
in the Middle-Eastern Theater.

Specialty Eyewear worn by Field Operatives on Light Patrols and Security Details. On top of having resistance against Flashes,
Users have found themselves recovering faster from concussive effects faster.

Highly Developmental Fork of Ballistic Goggles. Compared to the commericial varient, these goggles provide the maximum amount
of protection against grenade shrapnel and other debris.

Fully Realized Cross-Com Glasses, providing further information displays including the current status of it's user as well as an
integrated on-screen GPS System.

column1: Body Armor [Armored Rigs will be applied first in terms of durability.]

column5: Description + Effect

Basic Ballistic Vest for Law Enforcement Forces across the world. 
Stops small caliburs, but virtually useless against any sort of rifle. It's bad, but better than nothing. [Armor Class 1]

Company Issued Body Armor. 
Provides the most protection to Light Arms Fire and Shrapnel. Covers most of the upper body. [Armor Class 3]

Company Issued Body Armor. 
Provides the best movement ability at the cost of protection. Does not cover the stoamch. [Armor Class 3]

Company Issued Body Armor. 
Provides a good balance of protection and balance. Does not cover the stomach. Covers the shoulders. [Armor Class 3]

An all in one package for body armor, acting as both a carrier and a rig at the same time. Incompatible with Chest Rigs. 
Carries 8 mags, 2 grenades, and 2 spots for designated flares. [Armor Class 4]

Lightweight Plate Carrier popular with most PMCs.
Provides ample chest protection, while leaving the stomach exposed. [Armor Class 4]

Body Armor in use by the FSB. 
Known for it's optimized armor elements and special damping-ventilation system.
This vest is a good cost-effective pickup. [Armor Class 3]

Armored Vest previously in use by Russian Forces. 
Decent Protection and coverage, alongside lightweight materials. [Armor Class 3]

Main Armored Vest for European-based Contractors across Europe. 
Good Protection all-around and decent weight allows for ease of movement. [Armor Class 3]

Iniitally designed for Assualt Units in mind by the Ministry of Internal Affairs,.
This Ceramic Armored Vest will protect you substantially, all while being light to the user. [Armor Class 4]

Heavy Duty Armor that allows the user full rein over arm movement. 
Covers most of the chest region. Less heavier than it's counterpart. [Armor Class 4]

Heavy Duty Armor that allows the user full rein over arm movement. 
Covers the upper body. Extremely heavy, but covers most of the upper body. {Armor Class 4}

Hexagon Design Carrier enables the lightest weight to protection ratio. 
Does not cover the stomach. Intended use alongside chest rigs. [Armor Class 5]

Plate Carrier designed for maximum chest protection as well as superior weight. 
Does not cover the stomach. [Armor Class 5]

Bulletproof Vest designed for protection against light arm fire, fragmentation, mines, and shrapnel. 
Best in Protection Class, but extremely heavy. [Armor Class 5]

column1: Chest Rigs [Armored Rigs will be applied first in terms of durability.] [Small Pouches carry four small items.]

column5: Description + Effect

Company Issued Carrier Rig. 
Able to hold eight mags of any size alongside two grenades. 
The Standard Carrier Rig for the everyday occasion. [Armor Class 3]

Company Carrier Rig sent by order. 
Holds two mags, but is able to carry six LMG/HMG Boxes and four grenades instead. 
Extremely bulky in practicality's use. [Armor Class 3]

column1: Ars Arma A18 Skanda Carrier Rig
column5:
Carrier Rig that is also armored. 
Carries 12 mags, with 5 slots for flares and grenades. 
Utilized by Middle-Eastern PMCs operating in the sandbox. [Armor Class 4]

Chest Rig utilized the most by Shiori. 
Designed for Light-weight Insertions in mind. 
This chest rig enables the user quick access to six mags and grenades, with two side pouches enabling item priority for the user.

Unique Modular Carrier Rig that allows for different items to be stored in it's pouches. 
Standard eight mag carries or two LMG/HMG Boxes, with ample space for other items. 
[Max of Four Items] [Armor Class 4]

column1: Ars Arma CPC Mod.2 Carrier Rig
column5:
Upgraded Varient of the Ars Arma CPC. 
Carries 10 mags by design, room for 3 grenades, 2 flares, and pouch-bag for item acquisation. 
[Max of Four Items] [Armor Class 4]

Unarmored Chest Rig that leaves protection to the Armor Rig instead. 
Lightweight, and able to carry 12 mags, or 4 LMG Boxes.
Lightweight Chest Rig allowing 12 mags to be carried, with a small space for two grenades to be held on to for ease of use.

Standard Rig utilized by Avalon Operators.
Carries eight mags, with 2 grenade mounts, and a side flare mount for identifcation. Bought through After-Market Means.

Famous Chest Rig that saw usage during the Afghan War, adopted by the GRU in 1989. 
Carries six mags, four LMG Boxes and 10 pouches for VOG-25 or M67 Hand-Grenades. Actually really heavy.

column1:
Backpacks
[Small] = 12 Item Storage
[Standard] = 24 Item Storage
[Large] = 36 Item Storage

column5: Description + Effect

Company Issue Backpack for Short-Term Operations. 
The least amount of space to hold, but is rather light as a result. [Small Storage]

Company Standard Issue Backpack. 
Issued to everyone, and can be rebought should the need arise. [Standard Storage]

Company Issue Backpack for Extended Operations within the sector. 
The largest amount of space, but rather bulky and heavy. [Large Storage.]

column1: Direct Action Dragon Egg MK.II Pack
column5:
Special Operations Pack designed in mind for user preformance and endurance over long periods of time.
Lightweight and can carry ammo cartridges, additional mags, and medical supplies only. [Light Storage]

Operation Pack featuring generous single person storage.
Features a compartment for medical supplies. [Standard Storage]

Bulky backpack for extended AO durations.
Enough space to keep a camping kit and survival kits in, but not optimized for much else. [Large Storage]

PMC Backpack of choice.
Best overall storage in ratio to it's estimated weight. 
Good Upgrade from the company issued, at a steep cost. [Standard Storage.]

Large Scale Backpack intended for the first three days of intense fighting.
It's primary use is for the assurance of user survivability.
Features a luxerious amount of storage, for an insane weight trade off. [Large Storage]

Modular Backpack that can have mini-bags attached to it to increase it's storage even further.
Mini-bags sold at 1/4th the original price. [Standard Storage] [Large Storage when all four bags are attached.]
Lightweight Backpack made for comfort alongside user aerodynamics. [Small Storage]

Russian-made Field Backpack issued to Russian Military Forces.
An extremely reliable standard issue bag, with a primary focus on fire-arm scavanging. 
[Standard Storage with Two Weapon Sling Clips.]

Corpsmen issued backpack meant to hold medical supplies.
Can carry better medical supplies [Standard Storage]

Largest Backpack available, competing only towards the Three Day Assault Pack.
Looks alot like a sleeping roll, with the shared characteristic being heavy as shit. [Large Storage]
Description + Effect
Allows for mounting of NVGs.

Monocle NVG. Quite long and mostly used for vision rather than continued use. 
Batteries last twice as long with this equipment. Green Sight Picture.

Civilian Model NVGs, not as effective as the Military Model. 
Unique for it's Blue-ish Green Illumination.

Psuedo Binocular NVG, utilized by many PMCs for it's availability and user-friendliness. 
Features x8 Zoom in, with an adaquate green sight picture.
Allows for mounting of NVGs, with an ease of use compartment for the battery pack.

Highly Expensive Nighttime Optics Device or NODs. 
Feature panoramic view with it's unique design and is only utilized by the highest bidder. 
Only few are in circulation at the moment.

Unique Nightvision that shares the same expiremental design as the GPNVG-18. 
Can be utilized with the S10 NBC Gas-Mask.

column1: T-7 Thermal Goggles with NVG Mounts
column5:
Thermal Vision Goggles utilizing NVG Mounts. 
Displays in White-Hot Thermals. Beware of bright lights and hot sources.
